title: Apply assertWritable() to file-upload endpoint — closes /etc write bypass

verification.test: 316/316 workspace vitest (+3 new: U1 deny-list, U2 traversal, U3 happy-path)

origin: check-spec multi-agent review (2026-05-27) — Layer 2 Files/Terminal review flagged saveUpload() as the only mutating FilesService method that does not call assertWritable() after resolvePath().
rootCause: FilesService.saveUpload (apps/server/src/modules/files/files.service.ts:305-316) calls resolvePath(targetDir) but skips the assertWritable() deny-list check that every other mutating method (write/mkdir/rename/copyTo/remove/chmod/chown) applies. Result: any authenticated panel user can stream an HTTP upload directly into critical system paths (/etc/sudoers, /etc/ssh/sshd_config, /root/.ssh/authorized_keys) — escalating from panel-login to full host root.
fixSummary: One-line guard: call assertWritable(dir) immediately after resolvePath(targetDir), before fs.mkdir/pipeline. Plus a regression test that POSTs to /api/files/upload?path=/etc and asserts 403 FILE_FORBIDDEN_PATH.
